Lines.FullText: The Core Event is triggered on various changes to the openDAQ Components. This includes changes to property values,

Elements.RawText: The Core Event is triggered on various changes to the openDAQ Components. This includes changes to property values,

Lines.FullText: addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified

Elements.RawText: addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified

Lines.FullText: via the event ID available within the CoreEventArgs object. Each event type has a set of predetermined parameters

Lines.FullText: available in the `parameters` field of the arguments. These can be used by any openDAQ server, or other listener to

Lines.FullText: react to changes within the core structure.

Elements.RawText: react to changes within the core structure.

Description.RawText:
*
     * The Core Event is triggered on various changes to the openDAQ Components. This includes changes to property values,
     * addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified
     * via the event ID available within the CoreEventArgs object. Each event type has a set of predetermined parameters
     * available in the `parameters` field of the arguments. These can be used by any openDAQ server, or other listener to
     * react to changes within the core structure.

Lines.FullText: The Context serves as a container for the Scheduler and Logger. It originates

Elements.RawText: The Context serves as a container for the Scheduler and Logger. It originates

Lines.FullText: at the instance, and is passed to the root device, which forwards it to components

Elements.RawText: at the instance, and is passed to the root device, which forwards it to components

Lines.FullText: such as function blocks and signals.

Elements.RawText: such as function blocks and signals.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief The Context serves as a container for the Scheduler and Logger. It originates
 * at the instance, and is passed to the root device, which forwards it to components
 * such as function blocks and signals.

Lines.FullText: Note: The context holds a strong reference to the Module Manager until  the reference is moved via the

Elements.RawText: Note: The context holds a strong reference to the Module Manager until  the reference is moved via the

Lines.FullText: ContextInternal move function. The strong reference moved to an external owner to avoid memory leaks

Elements.RawText: ContextInternal move function. The strong reference moved to an external owner to avoid memory leaks

Lines.FullText: due to circular references. This is done automatically when the Context is used in the openDAQ Instance

Elements.RawText: due to circular references. This is done automatically when the Context is used in the openDAQ Instance

Lines.FullText: constructor.

Elements.RawText: constructor.

Description.RawText:
*
 * Note: The context holds a strong reference to the Module Manager until  the reference is moved via the
 * ContextInternal move function. The strong reference moved to an external owner to avoid memory leaks
 * due to circular references. This is done automatically when the Context is used in the openDAQ Instance
 * constructor.
